repo.description: Vim-fork focused on extensibility and usability
repo.name: neovim

body:
Drop the bundled busted and luacheck test dependency path in favor of a
repo-local Lua harness.

comment_data.body: replacing busted with ~2 files is extremely nice and lets us make progress towards simplifying busted, dropping its "magic", and being able to hook into the lifecycle.

Sneak yeet of `luacheck` ;) (I approve!)

Previously discussed at #32536
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -74,10 +74,6 @@ jobs:
         name: luals
         run: cmake --build build --target luals
 
-      - if: success() || failure() && steps.abort_job.outputs.status == 'success'
-        name: luacheck
-        run: cmake --build build --target lintlua-luacheck
-

comment_data.body:
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/da6b1bcf-02e4-4847-a58e-e160c193bd8b)

Good we don't need this cowboy programmed hack anymore
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -1,91 +0,0 @@
-local platform = vim.uv.os_uname()
-local deps_install_dir = table.remove(_G.arg, 1)
-_G.c_include_path = {}
-while vim.startswith(_G.arg[1], '-I') do
-  table.insert(_G.c_include_path, string.sub(table.remove(_G.arg, 1), 3))
-end
-local subcommand = table.remove(_G.arg, 1)
-local suffix = (platform and platform.sysname:lower():find 'windows') and '.dll' or '.so'
-package.path = (deps_install_dir .. '/?.lua;')
-  .. (deps_install_dir .. '/?/init.lua;')
-  .. package.path
-package.cpath = deps_install_dir .. '/?' .. suffix .. ';' .. package.cpath
-
-local uv = vim.uv
-
--- we use busted and luacheck and their lua dependencies
--- But installing their binary dependencies with luarocks is very
--- slow, replace them with vim.uv wrappers

comment_regular_data.body: Not a blocker, and not required for the MVP, but it would be extremely nice to expose this for plugins (which I assume should "just work", in which case it just needs a minimal documentation). I really don't want to keep https://github.com/nvim-treesitter/plentest.nvim around...
I want to expose something for plugins, but not everything. E.g. `screen.lua` needs some work before public exposure.
Yeah, plugins don't need the full infrastructure, just a lightweight harness that can replace busted/plenary.test_harness/plentest. It's also clearly not the primary driver for this change.
Looking at the code, it seems not clear what the distinction between a failure and an error is any more. Previously using `error()` in a test leads to an error, while now it leads to a failure.

I removed that distinction as I didn't see what value it added?

Can you explain to me why you would want to `fail` instead of `error`? Aren't they practically the same?
I missed changes in the reporter. Will remove the failure parts.
I don't understand what you are doing. I tested your PR and even explicit `error()` calls are reported as failures, so why are you trying to remove the failure reporting and keep the error reporting?

I added error modes back, but they can only be triggered outside of tests (`before_each` etc). `error()` in a test (`it()`) is still classed as a failure.

You seem to have strong opinions about this, so can you explain how you would want to model this? The main thing I wanted to simplify was calling `luassert.fail()` and `luassert.epicfail()` in some tests, which could just be replaced with `error()`. That allowed me to make `luassert.lua` stateless.

I'm mainly talking about the reporting of failures. The current behavior here doesn't look right:
```lua
function Reporter:summary_footer_noun(status, count)
  if status == 'error' then
    return count == 1 and 'ERROR' or 'ERRORS'
  end

  return count == 1 and 'TEST' or 'TESTS'
end
```
If there are 2 skipped tests and 1 test failure, at the end it shows
```
2 TESTS
1 TEST
```
which is very confusing.

Full summary I got in a run:
```
======== 200 tests from 1 test file of test/functional/plugin/lsp_spec.lua ran. (20020.24 ms total)
PASSED   197 tests.
SKIPPED  2 tests, listed below:
SKIPPED  ...ovim/build/Xtest_xdg/test/functional/plugin/lsp_spec.lua @ 1779: LSP basic_init test should check the body and didChange incremental normal mode editing
SKIPPED  ...ovim/build/Xtest_xdg/test/functional/plugin/lsp_spec.lua @ 6010: LSP vim.lsp._watchfiles sends notifications when files change (watchfunc=inotify)
inotify-tools not installed and not on CI
FAILED   1 test, listed below:
FAILED   ...ovim/build/Xtest_xdg/test/functional/plugin/lsp_spec.lua @ 5502: LSP cmd connects to lsp server via rpc.connect using hostname
...ovim/build/Xtest_xdg/test/functional/plugin/lsp_spec.lua:5502: Expected values to be equal.
Expected:
true
Actual:
false

stack traceback:
	[C]: in function 'error'
	/home/zeertzjq/dev/neovim/test/luassert.lua:28: in function 'eq'
	...ovim/build/Xtest_xdg/test/functional/plugin/lsp_spec.lua:76: in function 'verify_single_notification'
	...ovim/build/Xtest_xdg/test/functional/plugin/lsp_spec.lua:5502: in function <...ovim/build/Xtest_xdg/test/functional/plugin/lsp_spec.lua:5495>
	[C]: in function 'xpcall'
	/home/zeertzjq/dev/neovim/test/harness.lua:671: in function 'run_callable'
	/home/zeertzjq/dev/neovim/test/harness.lua:984: in function 'run_test'
	/home/zeertzjq/dev/neovim/test/harness.lua:1059: in function 'run_suite'
	/home/zeertzjq/dev/neovim/test/harness.lua:1057: in function 'run_suite'
	/home/zeertzjq/dev/neovim/test/harness.lua:1057: in function 'run_suite'
	/home/zeertzjq/dev/neovim/test/harness.lua:1057: in function 'run_suite'
	/home/zeertzjq/dev/neovim/test/harness.lua:1486: in function 'run_test_file'
	/home/zeertzjq/dev/neovim/test/harness.lua:1556: in function 'run_iteration'
	/home/zeertzjq/dev/neovim/test/harness.lua:1639: in function 'main'
	/home/zeertzjq/dev/neovim/test/runner.lua:30: in main chunk

 2 TESTS
 1 TEST
```

The test IDs also don't look right:
```
-------- Global test environment setup.
-------- Running tests from test/functional/api/autocmd_spec.lua
RUN       autocmd api nvim_create_autocmd validation: 20.31 ms OK
RUN      T1x autocmd api nvim_create_autocmd doesnt leak when you use ++once: 19.22 ms OK
RUN      T2x autocmd api nvim_create_autocmd allows passing buffer by key: 19.39 ms OK
RUN      T3x autocmd api nvim_create_autocmd does not allow passing invalid buffers: 17.70 ms OK
```
On this PR, the first test has no test ID, and all remain tests have an `x` suffix. Previously the `x` suffix is mainly used for skipped tests:
```lua
        -- Use a different test ID for skipped tests as well as Nvim instances spawned
        -- between this after_each() and the next before_each() (e.g. in setup()).
        _G._nvim_test_id = _G._nvim_test_id .. 'x'
```

title: test: replace busted with local harness
